{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Ōkunitama Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Ichinotorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|First torii}}]] '''Ōkunitama Shrine''' (おおくにたまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Taira Suganami|Q128846854|WD=Q128846854|1=Taira Suganami}}, {{ill|lt=Iwaki|Q274384|WD=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=small Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}} === History === [[File:Kabutoduka Kohun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)|Kabutozuka Kofun]]}}{{small|One theory views it as the tomb of {{ill|lt=Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11585422|WD=Q11585422|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}.}}]] According to shrine tradition, in the {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kanmu|Q314846|WD=Q314846|1=Emperor Kanmu}}, when Seii Taishōgun {{ill|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Q941979|WD=Q941979|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} passed through this area under imperial order to subdue the eastern barbarians, he found an old shrine badly devastated. After asking local people and learning it was Ōkunitama Shrine, he prayed for victory; after the enemy was pacified soon after, he built a large shrine in thanksgiving. Ancient ruins nearby include Nara-period temple remains and {{ill|lt=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)|Q17990175|WD=Q17990175|lt=Kabutozuka Kofun|1=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)}}. Because volume 10 "Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Honki" of '{{ill|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}}' includes "{{ill|lt=Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11585422|WD=Q11585422|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}," this area is inferred to have been their base, with this shrine considered their clan shrine and Kabutozuka Kofun their tomb. Also, {{ill|lt=Iwaki Province|Q1192809|WD=Q1192809|1=Iwaki Province (718)}} was established for several years from {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534|1=718}}), and this area is also considered a possible provincial capital site. Later, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it was ranked as small shrine. It was deeply revered by successive local lords, and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, donations of mikoshi, confirmation of documents, and construction of shrine buildings were successively made by the {{ill|lt=Naitō clan|Q2975325|WD=Q2975325|1=Naitō clan}} and {{ill|lt=Andō clan|Q11451352|WD=Q11451352|1=Andō clan}}, lords of {{ill|lt=Iwakitaira Domain|Q843782|WD=Q843782|1=Iwakitaira Domain}}. === Cultural properties === ; Kunitama Documents (Fukushima Prefecture designated Important Cultural Property) : Ancient documents from the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} to the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}. Together with the Yatsuki and Iino document groups, they are important medieval historical materials. Among them, documents bearing sleeve seals of {{ill|lt=Kitabatake Akiie|Q1036864|WD=Q1036864|1=Kitabatake Akiie}} and {{ill|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107|WD=Q297107|1=Ashikaga Takauji}}, and the "old genealogy" identifying the founder of the {{ill|lt=Iwaki clan|Q11474314|WD=Q11474314|1=Iwaki clan}}, are especially important. === Precinct === The honden is designated an Iwaki City tangible cultural property. File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Honden File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall === Transportation === * From {{ill|lt=Iwaki Station|Q5353428|WD=Q5353428|1=Iwaki Station (Fukushima)}} on the {{ill|lt=Jōban Line|Q1369263|WD=Q1369263|1=Jōban Line}}, take {{ill|lt=Shin Jōban Kōtsū|Q11502130|WD=Q11502130|1=Shin Joban Kotsu}} bus bound for Nishihara via Natsui, get off at "Ōkunitama Shrine" bus stop * 1 minute by car from the grade-separated junction of {{ill|lt=National Route 6|Q1038714|WD=Q1038714|1=Japan National Route 6}} {{ill|lt=Jōban Bypass|Q11481556|WD=Q11481556|1=Tokiwa bypass}} and {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefectural Route 229 Kabutozuka Kofun Line|Q11593467|WD=Q11593467|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 229}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11585422|WD=Q11585422|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} * {{ill|lt=Iwaki Province|Q1192809|WD=Q1192809|1=Iwaki Province (718)}} * {{ill|lt=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)|Q17990175|WD=Q17990175|1=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)}} * {{ill|lt=Iino Hachimangū|Q11666702|WD=Q11666702|1=Iino Hachimangū}} === External links === * [http://ookunitama.jp/ Ōkunitama Shrine] * [http://www.jp-spiritual.com/okunitama_jinjya1.htm Ōkunitama Shrine] (not official site, but detailed history) * [http://www.fukutabi.net/fuku/iwaki/ookuni.html Ōkunitama Shrine] - Fukushima fieldwork (townscape and historic architecture) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:福島県の旧県社]] [[Category:磐城平藩]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Among them, documents bearing sleeve seals of {{ill|lt=Kitabatake Akiie|Q1036864|WD=Q1036864|1=Kitabatake Akiie}} and {{ill|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107|WD=Q297107|1=Ashikaga Takauji}}, and the "old genealogy" identifying the founder of the {{ill|lt=Iwaki clan|Q11474314|WD=Q11474314|1=Iwaki clan}}, are especially important. === Precinct === The honden is designated an Iwaki City tangible cultural property. File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Honden File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall === Transportation === * From {{ill|lt=Iwaki Station|Q5353428|WD=Q5353428|1=Iwaki Station (Fukushima)}} on the {{ill|lt=Jōban Line|Q1369263|WD=Q1369263|1=Jōban Line}}, take {{ill|lt=Shin Jōban Kōtsū|Q11502130|WD=Q11502130|1=Shin Joban Kotsu}} bus bound for Nishihara via Natsui, get off at "Ōkunitama Shrine" bus stop * 1 minute by car from the grade-separated junction of {{ill|lt=National Route 6|Q1038714|WD=Q1038714|1=Japan National Route 6}} {{ill|lt=Jōban Bypass|Q11481556|WD=Q11481556|1=Tokiwa bypass}} and {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefectural Route 229 Kabutozuka Kofun Line|Q11593467|WD=Q11593467|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 229}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11585422|WD=Q11585422|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} * {{ill|lt=Iwaki Province|Q1192809|WD=Q1192809|1=Iwaki Province (718)}} * {{ill|lt=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)|Q17990175|WD=Q17990175|1=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)}} * {{ill|lt=Iino Hachimangū|Q11666702|WD=Q11666702|1=Iino Hachimangū}} === External links === * [http://ookunitama.jp/ Ōkunitama Shrine] * [http://www.jp-spiritual.com/okunitama_jinjya1.htm Ōkunitama Shrine] (not official site, but detailed history) * [http://www.fukutabi.net/fuku/iwaki/ookuni.html Ōkunitama Shrine] - Fukushima fieldwork (townscape and historic architecture) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:福島県の旧県社]] [[Category:磐城平藩]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

needed|date=March 2020}} {{神社 |名称 = Ōkunitama Shrine |画像 = [[File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Haiden.jpg|270px|]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Iwaki|Q274384|WD=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}{{ill|lt=Taira Suganami|Q128846854|WD=Q128846854|1=Taira Suganami}}Miyamae 54 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Ōkunitama Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Ichinotorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|First torii}}]] '''Ōkunitama Shrine''' (おおくにたまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Taira Suganami|Q128846854|WD=Q128846854|1=Taira Suganami}}, {{ill|lt=Iwaki|Q274384|WD=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=small Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}} === History === [[File:Kabutoduka Kohun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)|Kabutozuka Kofun]]}}{{small|One theory views it as the tomb of {{ill|lt=Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11585422|WD=Q11585422|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}.}}]] According to shrine tradition, in the {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kanmu|Q314846|WD=Q314846|1=Emperor Kanmu}}, when Seii Taishōgun {{ill|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Q941979|WD=Q941979|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} passed through this area under imperial order to subdue the eastern barbarians, he found an old shrine badly devastated. After asking local people and learning it was Ōkunitama Shrine, he prayed for victory; after the enemy was pacified soon after, he built a large shrine in thanksgiving. Ancient ruins nearby include Nara-period temple remains and {{ill|lt=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)|Q17990175|WD=Q17990175|lt=Kabutozuka Kofun|1=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)}}. Because volume 10 "Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Honki" of '{{ill|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}}' includes "{{ill|lt=Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11585422|WD=Q11585422|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}," this area is inferred to have been their base, with this shrine considered their clan shrine and Kabutozuka Kofun their tomb. Also, {{ill|lt=Iwaki Province|Q1192809|WD=Q1192809|1=Iwaki Province (718)}} was established for several years from {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534|1=718}}), and this area is also considered a possible provincial capital site. Later, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it was ranked as small shrine. It was deeply revered by successive local lords, and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, donations of mikoshi, confirmation of documents, and construction of shrine buildings were successively made by the {{ill|lt=Naitō clan|Q2975325|WD=Q2975325|1=Naitō clan}} and {{ill|lt=Andō clan|Q11451352|WD=Q11451352|1=Andō clan}}, lords of {{ill|lt=Iwakitaira Domain|Q843782|WD=Q843782|1=Iwakitaira Domain}}. === Cultural properties === ; Kunitama Documents (Fukushima Prefecture designated Important Cultural Property) : Ancient documents from the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} to the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}. Together with the Yatsuki and Iino document groups, they are important medieval historical materials. Among them, documents bearing sleeve seals of {{ill|lt=Kitabatake Akiie|Q1036864|WD=Q1036864|1=Kitabatake Akiie}} and {{ill|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107|WD=Q297107|1=Ashikaga Takauji}}, and the "old genealogy" identifying the founder of the {{ill|lt=Iwaki clan|Q11474314|WD=Q11474314|1=Iwaki clan}}, are especially important. === Precinct === The honden is designated an Iwaki City tangible cultural property. File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Honden File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall === Transportation === * From {{ill|lt=Iwaki Station|Q5353428|WD=Q5353428|1=Iwaki Station (Fukushima)}} on the {{ill|lt=Jōban Line|Q1369263|WD=Q1369263|1=Jōban Line}}, take {{ill|lt=Shin Jōban Kōtsū|Q11502130|WD=Q11502130|1=Shin Joban Kotsu}} bus bound for Nishihara via Natsui, get off at "Ōkunitama Shrine" bus stop * 1 minute by car from the grade-separated junction of {{ill|lt=National Route 6|Q1038714|WD=Q1038714|1=Japan National Route 6}} {{ill|lt=Jōban Bypass|Q11481556|WD=Q11481556|1=Tokiwa bypass}} and {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefectural Route 229 Kabutozuka Kofun Line|Q11593467|WD=Q11593467|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 229}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11585422|WD=Q11585422|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} * {{ill|lt=Iwaki Province|Q1192809|WD=Q1192809|1=Iwaki Province (718)}} * {{ill|lt=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)|Q17990175|WD=Q17990175|1=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)}} * {{ill|lt=Iino Hachimangū|Q11666702|WD=Q11666702|1=Iino Hachimangū}} === External links === * [http://ookunitama.jp/ Ōkunitama Shrine] * [http://www.jp-spiritual.com/okunitama_jinjya1.htm Ōkunitama Shrine] (not official site, but detailed history) * [http://www.fukutabi.net/fuku/iwaki/ookuni.html Ōkunitama Shrine] - Fukushima fieldwork (townscape and historic architecture) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:福島県の旧県社]] [[Category:磐城平藩]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Ōkunitama Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Ichinotorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|First torii}}]] '''Ōkunitama Shrine''' (おおくにたまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Taira Suganami|Q128846854|WD=Q128846854|1=Taira Suganami}}, {{ill|lt=Iwaki|Q274384|WD=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=small Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}} === History === [[File:Kabutoduka Kohun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)|Kabutozuka Kofun]]}}{{small|One theory views it as the tomb of {{ill|lt=Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11585422|WD=Q11585422|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}.}}]] According to shrine tradition, in the {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kanmu|Q314846|WD=Q314846|1=Emperor Kanmu}}, when Seii Taishōgun {{ill|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Q941979|WD=Q941979|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} passed through this area under imperial order to subdue the eastern barbarians, he found an old shrine badly devastated. After asking local people and learning it was Ōkunitama Shrine, he prayed for victory; after the enemy was pacified soon after, he built a large shrine in thanksgiving. Ancient ruins nearby include Nara-period temple remains and {{ill|lt=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)|Q17990175|WD=Q17990175|lt=Kabutozuka Kofun|1=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)}}. Because volume 10 "Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Honki" of '{{ill|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}}' includes "{{ill|lt=Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11585422|WD=Q11585422|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}," this area is inferred to have been their base, with this shrine considered their clan shrine and Kabutozuka Kofun their tomb. Also, {{ill|lt=Iwaki Province|Q1192809|WD=Q1192809|1=Iwaki Province (718)}} was established for several years from {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534|1=718}}), and this area is also considered a possible provincial capital site. Later, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it was ranked as small shrine. It was deeply revered by successive local lords, and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, donations of mikoshi, confirmation of documents, and construction of shrine buildings were successively made by the {{ill|lt=Naitō clan|Q2975325|WD=Q2975325|1=Naitō clan}} and {{ill|lt=Andō clan|Q11451352|WD=Q11451352|1=Andō clan}}, lords of {{ill|lt=Iwakitaira Domain|Q843782|WD=Q843782|1=Iwakitaira Domain}}. === Cultural properties === ; Kunitama Documents (Fukushima Prefecture designated Important Cultural Property) : Ancient documents from the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} to the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}. Together with the Yatsuki and Iino document groups, they are important medieval historical materials. Among them, documents bearing sleeve seals of {{ill|lt=Kitabatake Akiie|Q1036864|WD=Q1036864|1=Kitabatake Akiie}} and {{ill|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107|WD=Q297107|1=Ashikaga Takauji}}, and the "old genealogy" identifying the founder of the {{ill|lt=Iwaki clan|Q11474314|WD=Q11474314|1=Iwaki clan}}, are especially important. === Precinct === The honden is designated an Iwaki City tangible cultural property. File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Honden File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall === Transportation === * From {{ill|lt=Iwaki Station|Q5353428|WD=Q5353428|1=Iwaki Station (Fukushima)}} on the {{ill|lt=Jōban Line|Q1369263|WD=Q1369263|1=Jōban Line}}, take {{ill|lt=Shin Jōban Kōtsū|Q11502130|WD=Q11502130|1=Shin Joban Kotsu}} bus bound for Nishihara via Natsui, get off at "Ōkunitama Shrine" bus stop * 1 minute by car from the grade-separated junction of {{ill|lt=National Route 6|Q1038714|WD=Q1038714|1=Japan National Route 6}} {{ill|lt=Jōban Bypass|Q11481556|WD=Q11481556|1=Tokiwa bypass}} and {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefectural Route 229 Kabutozuka Kofun Line|Q11593467|WD=Q11593467|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 229}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11585422|WD=Q11585422|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} * {{ill|lt=Iwaki Province|Q1192809|WD=Q1192809|1=Iwaki Province (718)}} * {{ill|lt=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)|Q17990175|WD=Q17990175|1=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)}} * {{ill|lt=Iino Hachimangū|Q11666702|WD=Q11666702|1=Iino Hachimangū}} === External links === * [http://ookunitama.jp/ Ōkunitama Shrine] * [http://www.jp-spiritual.com/okunitama_jinjya1.htm Ōkunitama Shrine] (not official site, but detailed history) * [http://www.fukutabi.net/fuku/iwaki/ookuni.html Ōkunitama Shrine] - {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Ōkunitama Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Ichinotorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|First torii}}]] '''Ōkunitama Shrine''' (おおくにたまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Taira Suganami|Q128846854|WD=Q128846854|1=Taira Suganami}}, {{ill|lt=Iwaki|Q274384|WD=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=small Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}} === History === [[File:Kabutoduka Kohun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)|Kabutozuka Kofun]]}}{{small|One theory views it as the tomb of {{ill|lt=Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11585422|WD=Q11585422|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}.}}]] According to shrine tradition, in the {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kanmu|Q314846|WD=Q314846|1=Emperor Kanmu}}, when Seii Taishōgun {{ill|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Q941979|WD=Q941979|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} passed through this area under imperial order to subdue the eastern barbarians, he found an old shrine badly devastated. After asking local people and learning it was Ōkunitama Shrine, he prayed for victory; after the enemy was pacified soon after, he built a large shrine in thanksgiving. Ancient ruins nearby include Nara-period temple remains and {{ill|lt=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)|Q17990175|WD=Q17990175|lt=Kabutozuka Kofun|1=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)}}. Because volume 10 "Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Honki" of '{{ill|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}}' includes "{{ill|lt=Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11585422|WD=Q11585422|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}," this area is inferred to have been their base, with this shrine considered their clan shrine and Kabutozuka Kofun their tomb. Also, {{ill|lt=Iwaki Province|Q1192809|WD=Q1192809|1=Iwaki Province (718)}} was established for several years from {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534|1=718}}), and this area is also considered a possible provincial capital site. Later, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it was ranked as small shrine. It was deeply revered by successive local lords, and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, donations of mikoshi, confirmation of documents, and construction of shrine buildings were successively made by the {{ill|lt=Naitō clan|Q2975325|WD=Q2975325|1=Naitō clan}} and {{ill|lt=Andō clan|Q11451352|WD=Q11451352|1=Andō clan}}, lords of {{ill|lt=Iwakitaira Domain|Q843782|WD=Q843782|1=Iwakitaira Domain}}. === Cultural properties === ; Kunitama Documents (Fukushima Prefecture designated Important Cultural Property) : Ancient documents from the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} to the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}. Together with the Yatsuki and Iino document groups, they are important medieval historical materials. Among them, documents bearing sleeve seals of {{ill|lt=Kitabatake Akiie|Q1036864|WD=Q1036864|1=Kitabatake Akiie}} and {{ill|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107|WD=Q297107|1=Ashikaga Takauji}}, and the "old genealogy" identifying the founder of the {{ill|lt=Iwaki clan|Q11474314|WD=Q11474314|1=Iwaki clan}}, are especially important. === Precinct === The honden is designated an Iwaki City tangible cultural property. File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Honden File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall === Transportation === * From {{ill|lt=Iwaki Station|Q5353428|WD=Q5353428|1=Iwaki Station (Fukushima)}} on the {{ill|lt=Jōban Line|Q1369263|WD=Q1369263|1=Jōban Line}}, take {{ill|lt=Shin Jōban Kōtsū|Q11502130|WD=Q11502130|1=Shin Joban Kotsu}} bus bound for Nishihara via Natsui, get off at "Ōkunitama Shrine" bus stop * 1 minute by car from the grade-separated junction of {{ill|lt=National Route 6|Q1038714|WD=Q1038714|1=Japan National Route 6}} {{ill|lt=Jōban Bypass|Q11481556|WD=Q11481556|1=Tokiwa bypass}} and {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefectural Route 229 Kabutozuka Kofun Line|Q11593467|WD=Q11593467|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 229}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11585422|WD=Q11585422|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} * {{ill|lt=Iwaki Province|Q1192809|WD=Q1192809|1=Iwaki Province (718)}} * {{ill|lt=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)|Q17990175|WD=Q17990175|1=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)}} * {{ill|lt=Iino Hachimangū|Q11666702|WD=Q11666702|1=Iino Hachimangū}} === External links === * [http://ookunitama.jp/ Ōkunitama Shrine] * [http://www.jp-spiritual.com/okunitama_jinjya1.htm Ōkunitama Shrine] (not official site, but detailed history) * [http://www.fukutabi.net/fuku/iwaki/ookuni.html Ōkunitama Shrine] - needed|date=March 2020}} {{神社 |名称 = Ōkunitama Shrine |画像 = [[File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Haiden.jpg|270px|]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Iwaki|Q274384|WD=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}{{ill|lt=Taira Suganami|Q128846854|WD=Q128846854|1=Taira Suganami}}Miyamae 54 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Ōkunitama Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Ichinotorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|First torii}}]] '''Ōkunitama Shrine''' (おおくにたまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Taira Suganami|Q128846854|WD=Q128846854|1=Taira Suganami}}, {{ill|lt=Iwaki|Q274384|WD=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=small Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}} === History === [[File:Kabutoduka Kohun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)|Kabutozuka Kofun]]}}{{small|One theory views it as the tomb of {{ill|lt=Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11585422|WD=Q11585422|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}.}}]] According to shrine tradition, in the {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kanmu|Q314846|WD=Q314846|1=Emperor Kanmu}}, when Seii Taishōgun {{ill|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Q941979|WD=Q941979|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} passed through this area under imperial order to subdue the eastern barbarians, he found an old shrine badly devastated. After asking local people and learning it was Ōkunitama Shrine, he prayed for victory; after the enemy was pacified soon after, he built a large shrine in thanksgiving. Ancient ruins nearby include Nara-period temple remains and {{ill|lt=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)|Q17990175|WD=Q17990175|lt=Kabutozuka Kofun|1=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)}}. Because volume 10 "Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Honki" of '{{ill|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}}' includes "{{ill|lt=Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11585422|WD=Q11585422|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}," this area is inferred to have been their base, with this shrine considered their clan shrine and Kabutozuka Kofun their tomb. Also, {{ill|lt=Iwaki Province|Q1192809|WD=Q1192809|1=Iwaki Province (718)}} was established for several years from {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534|1=718}}), and this area is also considered a possible provincial capital site. Later, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it was ranked as small shrine. It was deeply revered by successive local lords, and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, donations of mikoshi, confirmation of documents, and construction of shrine buildings were successively made by the {{ill|lt=Naitō clan|Q2975325|WD=Q2975325|1=Naitō clan}} and {{ill|lt=Andō clan|Q11451352|WD=Q11451352|1=Andō clan}}, lords of {{ill|lt=Iwakitaira Domain|Q843782|WD=Q843782|1=Iwakitaira Domain}}. === Cultural properties === ; Kunitama Documents (Fukushima Prefecture designated Important Cultural Property) : Ancient documents from the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} to the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}. Together with the Yatsuki and Iino document groups, they are important medieval historical materials. Among them, documents bearing sleeve seals of {{ill|lt=Kitabatake Akiie|Q1036864|WD=Q1036864|1=Kitabatake Akiie}} and {{ill|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107|WD=Q297107|1=Ashikaga Takauji}}, and the "old genealogy" identifying the founder of the {{ill|lt=Iwaki clan|Q11474314|WD=Q11474314|1=Iwaki clan}}, are especially important. === Precinct === The honden is designated an Iwaki City tangible cultural property. File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Honden File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall === Transportation === * From {{ill|lt=Iwaki Station|Q5353428|WD=Q5353428|1=Iwaki Station (Fukushima)}} on the {{ill|lt=Jōban Line|Q1369263|WD=Q1369263|1=Jōban Line}}, take {{ill|lt=Shin Jōban Kōtsū|Q11502130|WD=Q11502130|1=Shin Joban Kotsu}} bus bound for Nishihara via Natsui, get off at "Ōkunitama Shrine" bus stop * 1 minute by car from the grade-separated junction of {{ill|lt=National Route 6|Q1038714|WD=Q1038714|1=Japan National Route 6}} {{ill|lt=Jōban Bypass|Q11481556|WD=Q11481556|1=Tokiwa bypass}} and {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefectural Route 229 Kabutozuka Kofun Line|Q11593467|WD=Q11593467|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 229}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11585422|WD=Q11585422|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} * {{ill|lt=Iwaki Province|Q1192809|WD=Q1192809|1=Iwaki Province (718)}} * {{ill|lt=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)|Q17990175|WD=Q17990175|1=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)}} * {{ill|lt=Iino Hachimangū|Q11666702|WD=Q11666702|1=Iino Hachimangū}} === External links === * [http://ookunitama.jp/ Ōkunitama Shrine] * [http://www.jp-spiritual.com/okunitama_jinjya1.htm Ōkunitama Shrine] (not official site, but detailed history) * [http://www.fukutabi.net/fuku/iwaki/ookuni.html Ōkunitama Shrine] - Fukushima fieldwork (townscape and historic architecture) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:福島県の旧県社]] [[Category:磐城平藩]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

Among them, documents bearing sleeve seals of {{ill|lt=Kitabatake Akiie|Q1036864|WD=Q1036864|1=Kitabatake Akiie}} and {{ill|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107|WD=Q297107|1=Ashikaga Takauji}}, and the "old genealogy" identifying the founder of the {{ill|lt=Iwaki clan|Q11474314|WD=Q11474314|1=Iwaki clan}}, are especially important. === Precinct === The honden is designated an Iwaki City tangible cultural property. File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Honden File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall === Transportation === * From {{ill|lt=Iwaki Station|Q5353428|WD=Q5353428|1=Iwaki Station (Fukushima)}} on the {{ill|lt=Jōban Line|Q1369263|WD=Q1369263|1=Jōban Line}}, take {{ill|lt=Shin Jōban Kōtsū|Q11502130|WD=Q11502130|1=Shin Joban Kotsu}} bus bound for Nishihara via Natsui, get off at "Ōkunitama Shrine" bus stop * 1 minute by car from the grade-separated junction of {{ill|lt=National Route 6|Q1038714|WD=Q1038714|1=Japan National Route 6}} {{ill|lt=Jōban Bypass|Q11481556|WD=Q11481556|1=Tokiwa bypass}} and {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefectural Route 229 Kabutozuka Kofun Line|Q11593467|WD=Q11593467|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 229}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11585422|WD=Q11585422|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} * {{ill|lt=Iwaki Province|Q1192809|WD=Q1192809|1=Iwaki Province (718)}} * {{ill|lt=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)|Q17990175|WD=Q17990175|1=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)}} * {{ill|lt=Iino Hachimangū|Q11666702|WD=Q11666702|1=Iino Hachimangū}} === External links === * [http://ookunitama.jp/ Ōkunitama Shrine] * [http://www.jp-spiritual.com/okunitama_jinjya1.htm Ōkunitama Shrine] (not official site, but detailed history) * [http://www.fukutabi.net/fuku/iwaki/ookuni.html Ōkunitama Shrine] - needed|date=March 2020}} {{神社 |名称 = Ōkunitama Shrine |画像 = [[File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Haiden.jpg|270px|]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Iwaki|Q274384|WD=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}{{ill|lt=Taira Suganami|Q128846854|WD=Q128846854|1=Taira Suganami}}Miyamae 54 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Ōkunitama Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Ichinotorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|First torii}}]] '''Ōkunitama Shrine''' (おおくにたまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Taira Suganami|Q128846854|WD=Q128846854|1=Taira Suganami}}, {{ill|lt=Iwaki|Q274384|WD=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=small Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}} === History === [[File:Kabutoduka Kohun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)|Kabutozuka Kofun]]}}{{small|One theory views it as the tomb of {{ill|lt=Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11585422|WD=Q11585422|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}.}}]] According to shrine tradition, in the {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kanmu|Q314846|WD=Q314846|1=Emperor Kanmu}}, when Seii Taishōgun {{ill|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Q941979|WD=Q941979|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} passed through this area under imperial order to subdue the eastern barbarians, he found an old shrine badly devastated. After asking local people and learning it was Ōkunitama Shrine, he prayed for victory; after the enemy was pacified soon after, he built a large shrine in thanksgiving. Ancient ruins nearby include Nara-period temple remains and {{ill|lt=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)|Q17990175|WD=Q17990175|lt=Kabutozuka Kofun|1=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)}}. Because volume 10 "Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Honki" of '{{ill|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}}' includes "{{ill|lt=Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11585422|WD=Q11585422|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}," this area is inferred to have been their base, with this shrine considered their clan shrine and Kabutozuka Kofun their tomb. Also, {{ill|lt=Iwaki Province|Q1192809|WD=Q1192809|1=Iwaki Province (718)}} was established for several years from {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534|1=718}}), and this area is also considered a possible provincial capital site. Later, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it was ranked as small shrine. It was deeply revered by successive local lords, and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, donations of mikoshi, confirmation of documents, and construction of shrine buildings were successively made by the {{ill|lt=Naitō clan|Q2975325|WD=Q2975325|1=Naitō clan}} and {{ill|lt=Andō clan|Q11451352|WD=Q11451352|1=Andō clan}}, lords of {{ill|lt=Iwakitaira Domain|Q843782|WD=Q843782|1=Iwakitaira Domain}}. === Cultural properties === ; Kunitama Documents (Fukushima Prefecture designated Important Cultural Property) : Ancient documents from the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} to the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}. Together with the Yatsuki and Iino document groups, they are important medieval historical materials. Among them, documents bearing sleeve seals of {{ill|lt=Kitabatake Akiie|Q1036864|WD=Q1036864|1=Kitabatake Akiie}} and {{ill|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107|WD=Q297107|1=Ashikaga Takauji}}, and the "old genealogy" identifying the founder of the {{ill|lt=Iwaki clan|Q11474314|WD=Q11474314|1=Iwaki clan}}, are especially important. === Precinct === The honden is designated an Iwaki City tangible cultural property. File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Honden File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall === Transportation === * From {{ill|lt=Iwaki Station|Q5353428|WD=Q5353428|1=Iwaki Station (Fukushima)}} on the {{ill|lt=Jōban Line|Q1369263|WD=Q1369263|1=Jōban Line}}, take {{ill|lt=Shin Jōban Kōtsū|Q11502130|WD=Q11502130|1=Shin Joban Kotsu}} bus bound for Nishihara via Natsui, get off at "Ōkunitama Shrine" bus stop * 1 minute by car from the grade-separated junction of {{ill|lt=National Route 6|Q1038714|WD=Q1038714|1=Japan National Route 6}} {{ill|lt=Jōban Bypass|Q11481556|WD=Q11481556|1=Tokiwa bypass}} and {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefectural Route 229 Kabutozuka Kofun Line|Q11593467|WD=Q11593467|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 229}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11585422|WD=Q11585422|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} * {{ill|lt=Iwaki Province|Q1192809|WD=Q1192809|1=Iwaki Province (718)}} * {{ill|lt=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)|Q17990175|WD=Q17990175|1=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)}} * {{ill|lt=Iino Hachimangū|Q11666702|WD=Q11666702|1=Iino Hachimangū}} === External links === * [http://ookunitama.jp/ Ōkunitama Shrine] * [http://www.jp-spiritual.com/okunitama_jinjya1.htm Ōkunitama Shrine] (not official site, but detailed history) * [http://www.fukutabi.net/fuku/iwaki/ookuni.html Ōkunitama Shrine] - Fukushima fieldwork (townscape and historic architecture) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:福島県の旧県社]] [[Category:磐城平藩]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]] [[Category:Qq but with jawiki]]

needed|date=March 2020}} {{神社 |名称 = Ōkunitama Shrine |画像 = [[File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Haiden.jpg|270px|]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Iwaki|Q274384|WD=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}{{ill|lt=Taira Suganami|Q128846854|WD=Q128846854|1=Taira Suganami}}Miyamae 54 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Ōkunitama Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Ichinotorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|First torii}}]] '''Ōkunitama Shrine''' (おおくにたまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Taira Suganami|Q128846854|WD=Q128846854|1=Taira Suganami}}, {{ill|lt=Iwaki|Q274384|WD=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=small Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}} === History === [[File:Kabutoduka Kohun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)|Kabutozuka Kofun]]}}{{small|One theory views it as the tomb of {{ill|lt=Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11585422|WD=Q11585422|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}.}}]] According to shrine tradition, in the {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kanmu|Q314846|WD=Q314846|1=Emperor Kanmu}}, when Seii Taishōgun {{ill|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Q941979|WD=Q941979|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} passed through this area under imperial order to subdue the eastern barbarians, he found an old shrine badly devastated. After asking local people and learning it was Ōkunitama Shrine, he prayed for victory; after the enemy was pacified soon after, he built a large shrine in thanksgiving. Ancient ruins nearby include Nara-period temple remains and {{ill|lt=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)|Q17990175|WD=Q17990175|lt=Kabutozuka Kofun|1=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)}}. Because volume 10 "Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Honki" of '{{ill|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}}' includes "{{ill|lt=Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11585422|WD=Q11585422|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}," this area is inferred to have been their base, with this shrine considered their clan shrine and Kabutozuka Kofun their tomb. Also, {{ill|lt=Iwaki Province|Q1192809|WD=Q1192809|1=Iwaki Province (718)}} was established for several years from {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534|1=718}}), and this area is also considered a possible provincial capital site. Later, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it was ranked as small shrine. It was deeply revered by successive local lords, and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, donations of mikoshi, confirmation of documents, and construction of shrine buildings were successively made by the {{ill|lt=Naitō clan|Q2975325|WD=Q2975325|1=Naitō clan}} and {{ill|lt=Andō clan|Q11451352|WD=Q11451352|1=Andō clan}}, lords of {{ill|lt=Iwakitaira Domain|Q843782|WD=Q843782|1=Iwakitaira Domain}}. === Cultural properties === ; Kunitama Documents (Fukushima Prefecture designated Important Cultural Property) : Ancient documents from the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} to the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}. Together with the Yatsuki and Iino document groups, they are important medieval historical materials. Among them, documents bearing sleeve seals of {{ill|lt=Kitabatake Akiie|Q1036864|WD=Q1036864|1=Kitabatake Akiie}} and {{ill|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107|WD=Q297107|1=Ashikaga Takauji}}, and the "old genealogy" identifying the founder of the {{ill|lt=Iwaki clan|Q11474314|WD=Q11474314|1=Iwaki clan}}, are especially important. === Precinct === The honden is designated an Iwaki City tangible cultural property. File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Honden File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall === Transportation === * From {{ill|lt=Iwaki Station|Q5353428|WD=Q5353428|1=Iwaki Station (Fukushima)}} on the {{ill|lt=Jōban Line|Q1369263|WD=Q1369263|1=Jōban Line}}, take {{ill|lt=Shin Jōban Kōtsū|Q11502130|WD=Q11502130|1=Shin Joban Kotsu}} bus bound for Nishihara via Natsui, get off at "Ōkunitama Shrine" bus stop * 1 minute by car from the grade-separated junction of {{ill|lt=National Route 6|Q1038714|WD=Q1038714|1=Japan National Route 6}} {{ill|lt=Jōban Bypass|Q11481556|WD=Q11481556|1=Tokiwa bypass}} and {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefectural Route 229 Kabutozuka Kofun Line|Q11593467|WD=Q11593467|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 229}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11585422|WD=Q11585422|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} * {{ill|lt=Iwaki Province|Q1192809|WD=Q1192809|1=Iwaki Province (718)}} * {{ill|lt=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)|Q17990175|WD=Q17990175|1=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)}} * {{ill|lt=Iino Hachimangū|Q11666702|WD=Q11666702|1=Iino Hachimangū}} === External links === * [http://ookunitama.jp/ Ōkunitama Shrine] * [http://www.jp-spiritual.com/okunitama_jinjya1.htm Ōkunitama Shrine] (not official site, but detailed history) * [http://www.fukutabi.net/fuku/iwaki/ookuni.html Ōkunitama Shrine] - Fukushima fieldwork (townscape and historic architecture) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:福島県の旧県社]] [[Category:磐城平藩]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]] [[Category:Qq but with jawiki]]

Ancient ruins nearby include Nara-period temple remains and {{ill|lt=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)|Q17990175|WD=Q17990175|lt=Kabutozuka Kofun|1=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)}}. Because volume 10 "Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Honki" of '{{ill|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}}' includes "{{ill|lt=Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11585422|WD=Q11585422|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}," this area is inferred to have been their base, with this shrine considered their clan shrine and Kabutozuka Kofun their tomb. Also, {{ill|lt=Iwaki Province|Q1192809|WD=Q1192809|1=Iwaki Province (718)}} was established for several years from {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534|1=718}}), and this area is also considered a possible provincial capital site. Later, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it was ranked as small shrine. It was deeply revered by successive local lords, and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, donations of mikoshi, confirmation of documents, and construction of shrine buildings were successively made by the {{ill|lt=Naitō clan|Q2975325|WD=Q2975325|1=Naitō clan}} and {{ill|lt=Andō clan|Q11451352|WD=Q11451352|1=Andō clan}}, lords of {{ill|lt=Iwakitaira Domain|Q843782|WD=Q843782|1=Iwakitaira Domain}}. === Cultural properties === ; Kunitama Documents (Fukushima Prefecture designated Important Cultural Property) : Ancient documents from the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} to the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}. Together with the Yatsuki and Iino document groups, they are important medieval historical materials. Among them, documents bearing sleeve seals of {{ill|lt=Kitabatake Akiie|Q1036864|WD=Q1036864|1=Kitabatake Akiie}} and {{ill|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107|WD=Q297107|1=Ashikaga Takauji}}, and the "old genealogy" identifying the founder of the {{ill|lt=Iwaki clan|Q11474314|WD=Q11474314|1=Iwaki clan}}, are especially important. === Precinct === The honden is designated an Iwaki City tangible cultural property. File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Honden File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall === Transportation === * From {{ill|lt=Iwaki Station|Q5353428|WD=Q5353428|1=Iwaki Station (Fukushima)}} on the {{ill|lt=Jōban Line|Q1369263|WD=Q1369263|1=Jōban Line}}, take {{ill|lt=Shin Jōban Kōtsū|Q11502130|WD=Q11502130|1=Shin Joban Kotsu}} bus bound for Nishihara via Natsui, get off at "Ōkunitama Shrine" bus stop * 1 minute by car from the grade-separated junction of {{ill|lt=National Route 6|Q1038714|WD=Q1038714|1=Japan National Route 6}} {{ill|lt=Jōban Bypass|Q11481556|WD=Q11481556|1=Tokiwa bypass}} and {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefectural Route 229 Kabutozuka Kofun Line|Q11593467|WD=Q11593467|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 229}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11585422|WD=Q11585422|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} * {{ill|lt=Iwaki Province|Q1192809|WD=Q1192809|1=Iwaki Province (718)}} * {{ill|lt=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)|Q17990175|WD=Q17990175|1=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)}} * {{ill|lt=Iino Hachimangū|Q11666702|WD=Q11666702|1=Iino Hachimangū}} === External links === * [http://ookunitama.jp/ Ōkunitama Shrine] * [http://www.jp-spiritual.com/okunitama_jinjya1.htm Ōkunitama Shrine] (not official site, but detailed history) * [http://www.fukutabi.net/fuku/iwaki/ookuni.html Ōkunitama Shrine] - Fukushima fieldwork (townscape and historic architecture) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:福島県の旧県社]] [[Category:磐城平藩]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:Qq but with jawiki]]

Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Iwaki|Q274384|WD=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}{{ill|lt=Taira Suganami|Q128846854|WD=Q128846854|1=Taira Suganami}}Miyamae 54 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Ōkunitama Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Ichinotorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|First torii}}]] '''Ōkunitama Shrine''' (おおくにたまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Taira Suganami|Q128846854|WD=Q128846854|1=Taira Suganami}}, {{ill|lt=Iwaki|Q274384|WD=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=small Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}} === History === [[File:Kabutoduka Kohun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)|Kabutozuka Kofun]]}}{{small|One theory views it as the tomb of {{ill|lt=Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11585422|WD=Q11585422|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}.}}]] According to shrine tradition, in the {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kanmu|Q314846|WD=Q314846|1=Emperor Kanmu}}, when Seii Taishōgun {{ill|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Q941979|WD=Q941979|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} passed through this area under imperial order to subdue the eastern barbarians, he found an old shrine badly devastated. After asking local people and learning it was Ōkunitama Shrine, he prayed for victory; after the enemy was pacified soon after, he built a large shrine in thanksgiving. Ancient ruins nearby include Nara-period temple remains and {{ill|lt=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)|Q17990175|WD=Q17990175|lt=Kabutozuka Kofun|1=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)}}. Because volume 10 "Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Honki" of '{{ill|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}}' includes "{{ill|lt=Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11585422|WD=Q11585422|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}," this area is inferred to have been their base, with this shrine considered their clan shrine and Kabutozuka Kofun their tomb. Also, {{ill|lt=Iwaki Province|Q1192809|WD=Q1192809|1=Iwaki Province (718)}} was established for several years from {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534|1=718}}), and this area is also considered a possible provincial capital site. Later, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it was ranked as small shrine. It was deeply revered by successive local lords, and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, donations of mikoshi, confirmation of documents, and construction of shrine buildings were successively made by the {{ill|lt=Naitō clan|Q2975325|WD=Q2975325|1=Naitō clan}} and {{ill|lt=Andō clan|Q11451352|WD=Q11451352|1=Andō clan}}, lords of {{ill|lt=Iwakitaira Domain|Q843782|WD=Q843782|1=Iwakitaira Domain}}. === Cultural properties === ; Kunitama Documents (Fukushima Prefecture designated Important Cultural Property) : Ancient documents from the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} to the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}. Together with the Yatsuki and Iino document groups, they are important medieval historical materials. Among them, documents bearing sleeve seals of {{ill|lt=Kitabatake Akiie|Q1036864|WD=Q1036864|1=Kitabatake Akiie}} and {{ill|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107|WD=Q297107|1=Ashikaga Takauji}}, and the "old genealogy" identifying the founder of the {{ill|lt=Iwaki clan|Q11474314|WD=Q11474314|1=Iwaki clan}}, are especially important. === Precinct === The honden is designated an Iwaki City tangible cultural property. File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Honden File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall === Transportation === * From {{ill|lt=Iwaki Station|Q5353428|WD=Q5353428|1=Iwaki Station (Fukushima)}} on the {{ill|lt=Jōban Line|Q1369263|WD=Q1369263|1=Jōban Line}}, take {{ill|lt=Shin Jōban Kōtsū|Q11502130|WD=Q11502130|1=Shin Joban Kotsu}} bus bound for Nishihara via Natsui, get off at "Ōkunitama Shrine" bus stop * 1 minute by car from the grade-separated junction of {{ill|lt=National Route 6|Q1038714|WD=Q1038714|1=Japan National Route 6}} {{ill|lt=Jōban Bypass|Q11481556|WD=Q11481556|1=Tokiwa bypass}} and {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefectural Route 229 Kabutozuka Kofun Line|Q11593467|WD=Q11593467|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 229}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11585422|WD=Q11585422|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} * {{ill|lt=Iwaki Province|Q1192809|WD=Q1192809|1=Iwaki Province (718)}} * {{ill|lt=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)|Q17990175|WD=Q17990175|1=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)}} * {{ill|lt=Iino Hachimangū|Q11666702|WD=Q11666702|1=Iino Hachimangū}} === External links === * [http://ookunitama.jp/ Ōkunitama Shrine] * [http://www.jp-spiritual.com/okunitama_jinjya1.htm Ōkunitama Shrine] (not official site, but detailed history) * [http://www.fukutabi.net/fuku/iwaki/ookuni.html Ōkunitama Shrine] - Fukushima fieldwork (townscape and historic architecture) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:福島県の旧県社]] [[Category:磐城平藩]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:Qq but with jawiki]]

needed|date=March 2020}} {{神社 |名称 = Ōkunitama Shrine |画像 = [[File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Haiden.jpg|270px|]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Iwaki|Q274384|WD=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}{{ill|lt=Taira Suganami|Q128846854|WD=Q128846854|1=Taira Suganami}}Miyamae 54 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Ōkunitama Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Ichinotorii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|First torii}}]] '''Ōkunitama Shrine''' (おおくにたまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Taira Suganami|Q128846854|WD=Q128846854|1=Taira Suganami}}, {{ill|lt=Iwaki|Q274384|WD=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=small Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}} === History === [[File:Kabutoduka Kohun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)|Kabutozuka Kofun]]}}{{small|One theory views it as the tomb of {{ill|lt=Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11585422|WD=Q11585422|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}.}}]] According to shrine tradition, in the {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kanmu|Q314846|WD=Q314846|1=Emperor Kanmu}}, when Seii Taishōgun {{ill|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Q941979|WD=Q941979|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} passed through this area under imperial order to subdue the eastern barbarians, he found an old shrine badly devastated. After asking local people and learning it was Ōkunitama Shrine, he prayed for victory; after the enemy was pacified soon after, he built a large shrine in thanksgiving. Ancient ruins nearby include Nara-period temple remains and {{ill|lt=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)|Q17990175|WD=Q17990175|lt=Kabutozuka Kofun|1=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)}}. Because volume 10 "Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Honki" of '{{ill|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}}' includes "{{ill|lt=Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11585422|WD=Q11585422|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}," this area is inferred to have been their base, with this shrine considered their clan shrine and Kabutozuka Kofun their tomb. Also, {{ill|lt=Iwaki Province|Q1192809|WD=Q1192809|1=Iwaki Province (718)}} was established for several years from {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534|1=718}}), and this area is also considered a possible provincial capital site. Later, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it was ranked as small shrine. It was deeply revered by successive local lords, and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, donations of mikoshi, confirmation of documents, and construction of shrine buildings were successively made by the {{ill|lt=Naitō clan|Q2975325|WD=Q2975325|1=Naitō clan}} and {{ill|lt=Andō clan|Q11451352|WD=Q11451352|1=Andō clan}}, lords of {{ill|lt=Iwakitaira Domain|Q843782|WD=Q843782|1=Iwakitaira Domain}}. === Cultural properties === ; Kunitama Documents (Fukushima Prefecture designated Important Cultural Property) : Ancient documents from the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} to the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}. Together with the Yatsuki and Iino document groups, they are important medieval historical materials. Among them, documents bearing sleeve seals of {{ill|lt=Kitabatake Akiie|Q1036864|WD=Q1036864|1=Kitabatake Akiie}} and {{ill|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107|WD=Q297107|1=Ashikaga Takauji}}, and the "old genealogy" identifying the founder of the {{ill|lt=Iwaki clan|Q11474314|WD=Q11474314|1=Iwaki clan}}, are especially important. === Precinct === The honden is designated an Iwaki City tangible cultural property. File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Honden File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall === Transportation === * From {{ill|lt=Iwaki Station|Q5353428|WD=Q5353428|1=Iwaki Station (Fukushima)}} on the {{ill|lt=Jōban Line|Q1369263|WD=Q1369263|1=Jōban Line}}, take {{ill|lt=Shin Jōban Kōtsū|Q11502130|WD=Q11502130|1=Shin Joban Kotsu}} bus bound for Nishihara via Natsui, get off at "Ōkunitama Shrine" bus stop * 1 minute by car from the grade-separated junction of {{ill|lt=National Route 6|Q1038714|WD=Q1038714|1=Japan National Route 6}} {{ill|lt=Jōban Bypass|Q11481556|WD=Q11481556|1=Tokiwa bypass}} and {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefectural Route 229 Kabutozuka Kofun Line|Q11593467|WD=Q11593467|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 229}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11585422|WD=Q11585422|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} * {{ill|lt=Iwaki Province|Q1192809|WD=Q1192809|1=Iwaki Province (718)}} * {{ill|lt=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)|Q17990175|WD=Q17990175|1=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)}} * {{ill|lt=Iino Hachimangū|Q11666702|WD=Q11666702|1=Iino Hachimangū}} === External links === * [http://ookunitama.jp/ Ōkunitama Shrine] * [http://www.jp-spiritual.com/okunitama_jinjya1.htm Ōkunitama Shrine] (not official site, but detailed history) * [http://www.fukutabi.net/fuku/iwaki/ookuni.html Ōkunitama Shrine] - Fukushima fieldwork (townscape and historic architecture) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:福島県の旧県社]] [[Category:磐城平藩]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:Qq but with jawiki]]

Because volume 10 "Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Honki" of '{{ill|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}}' includes "{{ill|lt=Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11585422|WD=Q11585422|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}," this area is inferred to have been their base, with this shrine considered their clan shrine and Kabutozuka Kofun their tomb. Also, {{ill|lt=Iwaki Province|Q1192809|WD=Q1192809|1=Iwaki Province (718)}} was established for several years from {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534|1=718}}), and this area is also considered a possible provincial capital site. Later, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it was ranked as small shrine. It was deeply revered by successive local lords, and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, donations of mikoshi, confirmation of documents, and construction of shrine buildings were successively made by the {{ill|lt=Naitō clan|Q2975325|WD=Q2975325|1=Naitō clan}} and {{ill|lt=Andō clan|Q11451352|WD=Q11451352|1=Andō clan}}, lords of {{ill|lt=Iwakitaira Domain|Q843782|WD=Q843782|1=Iwakitaira Domain}}. === Cultural properties === ; Kunitama Documents (Fukushima Prefecture designated Important Cultural Property) : Ancient documents from the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}} to the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}. Together with the Yatsuki and Iino document groups, they are important medieval historical materials. Among them, documents bearing sleeve seals of {{ill|lt=Kitabatake Akiie|Q1036864|WD=Q1036864|1=Kitabatake Akiie}} and {{ill|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107|WD=Q297107|1=Ashikaga Takauji}}, and the "old genealogy" identifying the founder of the {{ill|lt=Iwaki clan|Q11474314|WD=Q11474314|1=Iwaki clan}}, are especially important. === Precinct === The honden is designated an Iwaki City tangible cultural property. File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Honden File:Okunitama Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall === Transportation === * From {{ill|lt=Iwaki Station|Q5353428|WD=Q5353428|1=Iwaki Station (Fukushima)}} on the {{ill|lt=Jōban Line|Q1369263|WD=Q1369263|1=Jōban Line}}, take {{ill|lt=Shin Jōban Kōtsū|Q11502130|WD=Q11502130|1=Shin Joban Kotsu}} bus bound for Nishihara via Natsui, get off at "Ōkunitama Shrine" bus stop * 1 minute by car from the grade-separated junction of {{ill|lt=National Route 6|Q1038714|WD=Q1038714|1=Japan National Route 6}} {{ill|lt=Jōban Bypass|Q11481556|WD=Q11481556|1=Tokiwa bypass}} and {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefectural Route 229 Kabutozuka Kofun Line|Q11593467|WD=Q11593467|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 229}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11585422|WD=Q11585422|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} * {{ill|lt=Iwaki Province|Q1192809|WD=Q1192809|1=Iwaki Province (718)}} * {{ill|lt=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)|Q17990175|WD=Q17990175|1=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)}} * {{ill|lt=Iino Hachimangū|Q11666702|WD=Q11666702|1=Iino Hachimangū}} === External links === * [http://ookunitama.jp/ Ōkunitama Shrine] * [http://www.jp-spiritual.com/okunitama_jinjya1.htm Ōkunitama Shrine] (not official site, but detailed history) * [http://www.fukutabi.net/fuku/iwaki/ookuni.html Ōkunitama Shrine] - Fukushima fieldwork (townscape and historic architecture) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:福島県の旧県社]] [[Category:磐城平藩]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwaki, Fukushima]]
